Course Schedule

This page contains a schedule of the topics, content, and assignments for the semester. Note that this schedule will be updated as necessary the semester progresses, with all changes documented here. Readings requiring institutional subscriptions can be accessed through the Cornell Library.

Week Date Topic Slides Lab HW Reading
Module 1: Introduction to Systems Analysis
1 Mon, Aug 25 Welcome to BEE 4750!
Wed, Aug 27 Lab: Setting up Julia and GitHub
2 Mon, Sep 01 No Class: Labor Day Liu et al. (2007)
Wed, Sep 03 Introduction to Systems Models
3 Mon, Sep 08 System Dynamics: Feedbacks Woodard et al. (2019)
Wed, Sep 10 Equilibria and Bifurcations
4 Mon, Sep 15 Simulation Models Oreskes et al. (1994)
Wed, Sep 17 Dissolved Oxygen
5 Mon, Sep 22 More Dissolved Oxygen and Simulation Simpson et al. (2021)
Wed, Sep 24 Uncertainty and Monte Carlo
6 Mon, Sep 29 Monte Carlo: Why It Works
Wed, Oct 01 Lab: Monte Carlo
7 Mon, Oct 06 Prescriptive Modeling
Wed, Oct 08 Prelim 1
8 Mon, Oct 13 No Class: Fall Break
Wed, Oct 15 Decision Models and Optimization
9 Mon, Oct 20 Shadow Prices and Capacity Expansion
Wed, Oct 22 Lab: Linear Programming
10 Mon, Oct 27 Prelim Review and Capacity Expansion II
Wed, Oct 29 Economic Dispatch
11 Mon, Nov 03 Fixed Costs and Mixed Integer Programs
Wed, Nov 05 No Class
12 Mon, Nov 10 Network Models and Solid Waste Management
Wed, Nov 12 Prelim 2
13 Mon, Nov 17 Plume Dispersion Models
Wed, Nov 19 Managing Sources of Air Pollution
14 Mon, Nov 24 Stochastic Optimization
Wed, Nov 26 No Class: Thanksgiving Break
15 Mon, Dec 01 Prelim II Review and Sensitivity Analysis
Wed, Dec 03 Limits of Optimization
16 Mon, Dec 08 No Class: Project Work

References

Liu, J., Dietz, T., Carpenter, S. R., Alberti, M., Folke, C., Moran, E., et al. (2007). Complexity of coupled human and natural systems. Science, 317, 1513–1516. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.1144004
Oreskes, N., Shrader-Frechette, K., & Belitz, K. (1994). Verification, validation, and confirmation of numerical models in the Earth sciences. Science, 263, 641–646. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.263.5147.641
Simpson, N. P., Mach, K. J., Constable, A., Hess, J., Hogarth, R., Howden, M., et al. (2021). A framework for complex climate change risk assessment. One Earth, 4, 489–501. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.oneear.2021.03.005
Woodard, D. L., Davis, S. J., & Randerson, J. T. (2019). Economic carbon cycle feedbacks may offset additional warming from natural feedbacks. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, 116, 759–764. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1805187115

2025 Vivek Srikrishnan
See the About Page for credit information.
